sourceMovie.title: The Strangers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Strangers works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Halloween
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Strangers clicked for you, Halloween (1978) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Strangers?
faq.answer: Try Halloween, Halloween, Scream VI—each shares DNA with The Strangers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Strangers?
faq.answer: The Strangers (2008) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Strangers part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Strangers Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
